content.heading: The Brief
content.body: A multinational NGO expanding its East African operations needed a Kampala office that could accommodate 45 staff, three conference rooms, and dedicated visitor-facing areas — all within a leased floor plate that required structural sensitivity and a tight delivery timeline.

content.caption: The open-plan working floor — zoned acoustically without full-height partitions.

items.heading: Workplace Philosophy
items.body: The layout rejects the traditional cell-office model in favour of activity-based working: quiet focus areas, collaborative benches, phone booths, and informal breakout nodes. Staff are not assigned fixed desks.

items.heading: Brand Integration
items.body: The organisation's identity — built around transparency and community — is expressed through open sightlines, sustainable material choices, and locally-sourced artwork throughout. Nothing is purely decorative; everything reinforces the mission.

content.heading: Delivery
content.body: Designed, permitted, and handed over in eleven weeks. The client was operational on day one of occupation with zero snagging items outstanding — a first in our project history.
